layer3_inference: Signal 014 — Cross-jurisdictional emergency water supply needs / mutual aid
rejection_reason: Layer 3 correctly identified this as unscorable. Layer 4 confirms: seven corrections ledger entries have stripped this inference class. The signal is built entirely on 'what would logically need to happen' without any real-world evidence. Per LZ-CC-001, internal logical consistency without real-world anchoring is not evidence. Layer 3 did not attempt to score this — it was already stripped. Layer 4 confirms the strip.

layer3_inference: Signal 010 — Fire department forced to alternative water sourcing (behavioral inference about tanker operations)
rejection_reason: Layer 3 correctly classified the behavioral inference as SPECULATIVE with INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE. The underlying hydrant data (0 PSI) is valid but already fully captured in Signal 003. The fire dispatch behavioral layer adds zero independently verifiable information. Layer 4 reduces final composite to 0 — the signal's independent contribution is nil after removing the unsupported behavioral inference and the duplicative hydrant data.
